danger_signals.visual_form: Protruding spikes visible on floors and walls
danger_signals.meaning: Instant death hazard; visual clarity prevents accidental contact

danger_signals.visual_form: Bottomless vertical gaps spanning three or more story heights
danger_signals.meaning: Fatal fall hazard; depth emphasized through environmental perspective

danger_signals.visual_form: Falling blade mechanisms with visible activation zones
danger_signals.meaning: Timed crushing hazard; moving blade communicates danger window

danger_signals.visual_form: Reflective surface anomaly in dungeon environment paired with audio cue
danger_signals.meaning: Narrative trap; visual and sonic synchronization signals supernatural threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Armed guards and Jaffar rendered with sword and combat stance
danger_signals.meaning: Combat encounter; weapon visibility and posture communicate threat level

danger_signals.visual_form: Undead skeletal form animated as living combatant
danger_signals.meaning: Supernatural enemy; visual manifestation of curse/magic threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Shadow duplicate of protagonist emerging from mirror
danger_signals.meaning: Supernatural antagonist; visual identity with protagonist signals curse mechanic

danger_signals.signal: Red health indicator triangles at low count
danger_signals.visual_form: Diminishing row of red triangular UI elements
danger_signals.meaning: Cumulative damage state; visual density communicates proximity to death

danger_signals.visual_form: Potion containers distinguished by blue coloration
danger_signals.meaning: Poison trap disguised as healing item; color inversion signals danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Descending debris visible in architectural spaces
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazard; visual trajectory communicates collision threat

safety_signals.visual_form: Small containers with red coloration scattered throughout dungeon
safety_signals.meaning: Health restoration; warm color signals healing resource
Maximum health expansion; size escalation indicates enhanced benefit
Temporary invulnerability/mobility boost; green signals positive state enhancement

safety_signals.visual_form: Floor pressure plates or marked zones paired with corresponding gates
safety_signals.meaning: Passage enabler; visual connection between trigger and gate communicates mechanical safety

safety_signals.visual_form: Imprisoned female character visible through barriers, sends white mouse aid
safety_signals.meaning: Objective goal and passive ally; presence indicates progress and support

safety_signals.visual_form: Small white animal triggering gates on behalf of Princess
safety_signals.meaning: Ally communication system; appearance signals Princess is actively helping

safety_signals.visual_form: Obtainable melee weapon rendered in dungeon space
safety_signals.meaning: Player empowerment for combat encounters; visual access signals readiness

safety_signals.visual_form: Gate structures allowing progression to new level zones
safety_signals.meaning: Safe progression; passage through marks world advancement and relative safety

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Unnamed hero in light clothing (rotoscoped human figure)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Doppelgänger shadow version shares identical silhouette but represents corruption
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Free movement through dungeon spaces; sword as primary visual marker of player agency

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Armed swordsman with authoritative combat posture; final boss visual distinction
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Appears as primary human enemy type; guards serve as visual variants of his force
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Fortress tower as seat of power; magic mirror as signature supernatural tool; castle architecture represents his domain

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Armed swordsmen visually identical to combat-capable enemies
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Standard guards vs. Jaffar's elite status through positioning and encounter context
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Distributed throughout dungeon levels; guard placement indicates Jaffar's control over fortress spaces

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Imprisoned female character; passive role in fortress tower
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Not rendered as enemy; represents inverse faction to Jaffar
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Confinement behind barriers signals her status as captive; white mouse as her visible agent of assistance

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Magic mirror, skeletal swordsman, doppelgänger apparition, upside-down world display
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Supernatural entities manifest as visual anomalies distinct from physical guards
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Mirror chambers, magical effects (screen inversion), curse manifestations distributed through later levels

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Doppelgänger shadow persistent in world; visual splitting of protagonist identity
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Doppelgänger steals potions (Level 5) and throws protagonist into lower dungeon (Level 6-7); active antagonism demonstrates curse mechanics

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Female character visible behind barriers; confinement visualized through environmental separation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Princess remains passive until protagonist reaches final level; sends white mouse ally when protagonist trapped (Level 8), demonstrating awareness and limited agency

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Magical anomalies throughout levels: mirror portals, skeletal reanimation, doppelgänger persistence, inverted perspective (Level 9)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies behave with supernatural intelligence; environmental hazards respond to magical principles rather than physics; time pressure (60/120 minute countdown) represents spell's temporal constraint

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Red health indicator triangle count dramatically reduced at bottom UI
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Single hit from any source results in death; visual indicator density creates tension through visual scarcity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: In-game timer approaching zero (visible but specific numbers not emphasized in design)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Princess becomes unreachable if timer expires; game over state triggered; final level allows timer pause once Jaffar defeated

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Shadow and body unified; visual reconciliation of split self
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Invisible bridge becomes traversable only after merge; access to Jaffar unlocked; final confrontation enabled through unity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Antagonist rendered inert; curse visually dissolved
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Spell broken; Princess freed; timer stops at moment of Jaffar's death; final checkpoint reached prevents further game over

environmental_storytelling.example: Twelve sequential levels progressing from lower dungeons toward fortress tower; spatial architecture communicates narrative journey from captivity to confrontation with Jaffar

environmental_storytelling.technique: Potion color coding as communicative system
environmental_storytelling.example: Red potions restore health (warm/positive), blue potions poison (cold/negative/inverted meaning), green potions grant abilities; color language replaces text for resource literacy

environmental_storytelling.technique: Mirror as plot device and visual metaphor
environmental_storytelling.visual_form: Magic mirror encounter in Level 4; ominous leitmotif audio paired with reflection anomaly
environmental_storytelling.example: Doppelgänger emergence visualizes curse manifestation; mirror becomes recurring visual symbol of protagonist's internal conflict through Levels 5-6-12

environmental_storytelling.technique: NPC spatial positioning signaling allegiance and narrative role
environmental_storytelling.example: Princess confinement in tower communicates her antagonist status; white mouse free movement represents her will operating through small agent; guard distribution shows Jaffar's control zones

environmental_storytelling.technique: Trap placement density indicating dungeon severity
environmental_storytelling.example: Earlier levels sparse in traps; later levels accumulate spike/guillotine/pit combinations; visual complexity escalates narrative tension toward final confrontation

environmental_storytelling.technique: Skeletal swordsman as visual representation of cursed minion
environmental_storytelling.example: Undead guard cannot be killed by sword (normal rules broken); must be pit-dropped; visual otherness communicates magical corruption distinct from human guards

environmental_storytelling.technique: Screen inversion (Level 9) as magical reality distortion
environmental_storytelling.example: Upside-down world display manifests Jaffar's spell power visually; temporary reversal potion restores normal perspective; visual inversion = narrative magical intensity

environmental_storytelling.technique: Health indicator as visual resource depletion language
environmental_storytelling.example: Red triangle count visible at all times; diminishing triangles create visual urgency without requiring numeric literacy; immediate visual feedback for damage state

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Engage protagonist with sword maneuvers (advance, slash, parry, combined attacks); possess individual health indicators; can be defeated via sword attrition or pit-falling; attack patterns signal combat difficulty
Boss-level combat encounter; serves as ultimate gate-keeper to Princess; defeat triggers spell dissolution and victory state
Cannot be killed via normal sword combat; requires pit-drop for defeat; behaves as supernatural variant of guard type; represents magical curse manifestation

Steals potions from protagonist (Level 5); throws protagonist from level 6 into dungeon (Level 7); shares health with protagonist in Level 12 (any damage mutual); must be merged with rather than defeated; represents internal antagonism

Remains confined until final level; sends white mouse to trigger gate in Level 8 when protagonist trapped; demonstrates communication and limited agency through surrogate messenger; waits passively for rescue

Activated on behalf of Princess; travels through dungeon to trigger specific gates; represents Princess's will extended beyond her confinement; appears only when protagonist mechanically needs assistance

Takes damage from traps/enemies/falls (indicated by triangle loss); absorbs potion effects (red heals, blue poisons, green grants abilities); acquires sword in Level 1 enabling combat; animation reflects rotoscoped realistic movement; must complete 12 levels within time limit

notes: Prince of Persia (1989) demonstrates sophisticated visual communication through color-coded resources, spatial progression architecture, and supernatural visual anomalies. The game communicates world-state primarily through visual density (health triangles), environmental design (trap escalation), and character/entity placement rather than explicit text or numbers. Faction identity is expressed through physical positioning (protagonist free, Princess confined, guards distributed, Jaffar isolated in tower) and supernatural phenomena (mirror, skeleton, doppelgänger) signal magical power and curse progression. The magic mirror serves as central visual metaphor linking narrative theme (internal conflict/duality) to gameplay mechanic (doppelgänger obstacle). Time pressure and potion scarcity create visual tension through UI element scarcity. The game's rotoscoped animation prioritizes realistic movement communication over fantasy stylization, grounding danger/safety contrast in kinetic clarity.
